items.q: What is hexadecimal A in denary?
items.why: Students forget hex uses A-F to represent 10-15 because we need 16 distinct symbols. A=10, B=11, C=12, D=13, E=14, F=15.

items.q: How many layers are in the OSI model?
items.correct: 7 (Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application).
items.why: Students confuse OSI (7 layers) with the TCP/IP model (4 layers). They are related but distinct - OSI is a theoretical reference model; TCP/IP is the practical Internet stack.

items.q: What is the worst-case complexity of bubble sort?
items.misconception: O(n) because it just passes through the list.
items.why: Students count one pass and forget that bubble sort does up to n passes, each comparing up to n elements. The nested loop structure gives n times n = n squared.
Correct answer: does model match reality. “is code syntax correct” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Exact answer: 1. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
